description_long: This object is a small, pale crustacean with a segmented body and a hard exoskeleton. It features a prominent pair of large claws (chelae) at the front, which are textured and slightly darker than the rest of the body. The body is divided into a cephalothorax and a long, flexible abdomen, with several pairs of walking legs extending from the thoracic region. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the head, and long, thin antennae are visible extending forward. The coloration is mostly light tan to beige, with some subtle brownish markings and a slightly translucent quality to the exoskeleton.
description: A small, pale crustacean with large claws, segmented body, and visible antennae. Its exoskeleton is light tan with subtle brown markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, large claw, and extended walking legs.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the segmented abdomen and tail fan, with legs and claws partially visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile, including the second claw and the curvature of the body.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the large claws, head, eyes on stalks, and forward-projecting antennae.
